title: Jittery Newborn - Neonatal Hypoglycemia
chief_complaint: 6-hour-old infant born to diabetic mother (LGA, 4.2kg). Jittery, irritable, and poor feeding. Blood glucose check shows 28 mg/dL.

physical_exam.general: Jittery, exaggerated Moro reflex, irritable cry

key_learning_points: Screen glucose at 1, 2, 4, 6 hours of life
Treat with early feeding or IV dextrose if severe

patient_responses.mother: Mother has gestational diabetes, poorly controlled
patient_responses.feeding: She's not latching well, seems too jittery

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Hypoglycemia in IDM from hyperinsulinemia.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is neonatal hypoglycemia. The diabetic mother + LGA + low glucose confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Neonatal hypoglycemia: If BG <40 mg/dL and symptomatic, IV D10W 2mL/kg bolus then continuous infusion at 6-8mg/kg/min. If asymptomatic, encourage early feeding. Monitor glucose q1h until stable. Most resolve in 24-48h.
